title: ESCALATE: Old fetch script exposes raw exception text in bridge file

text: {"type":"escalate","finding":"Old fetch script exposes raw exception text","severity":"Important","description":"Deprecated tmux-status-quota-fetch:278 catches generic exceptions and writes str(e) into the bridge file's error field. Can expose file paths, network errors, Python tracebacks into the JSON cache. The new server scraper avoids this by using machine-readable codes only.","options":[{"label":"Option 1: Replace str(e) with machine-readable code","solution":"Change 'error': str(e) to 'error': 'fetch_error' to match server's pattern.","pros":"Consistent error handling, no information leakage","cons":"Harder to debug errors from this deprecated script"},{"label":"Option 2: Rely on TS-35 (stop shipping the script)","solution":"If the script is removed from install.sh (TS-35), this issue is moot for new installs.","pros":"No code change to deprecated file","cons":"Doesn't fix issue if someone runs it manually"}],"recommendation":"Option 2 if TS-35 is accepted, otherwise Option 1","promotion_reason":"Natural FIX promoted to ESCALATE — max fix cycles (3) reached after 4 cycles"}
